meaning: adj.戴头巾的,包着头巾的,[动]头帕海胆
examples: Turbaned men talked agitatedly outside among parked pick - up trucks and military vehicles.
Today the American Marine captain only has to step out of his base to be overwhelmed by turbaned men anxious to be his best friend.
Slim turbaned indians blew through long pipes of reed or brass and charmed-or feigned to charm-great hooded snakes and horrible horned adders.
Carpet-bagging politicians rolled up in Mercedes cars with tinted windows, alongside turbaned Shia clerics and baggy-trousered Druze elders.
Among the men patrolling the streets are Arabic-speaking turbaned Chadians, though it is not clear that Chad's government approves their presence.
